clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Screen for primary aldosteronism in resistant hypertension regardless of potassium levels
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults with resistant hypertension, including those without hypokalemia
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Increase detection, diagnosis, and targeted therapy for primary aldosteronism

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for resistant hypertension evaluation
Confirm adult patient with resistant hypertension
Note: screening recommended regardless of hypokalemia status
Perform aldosterone-to-renin ratio or other screening test
Confirm primary aldosteronism with confirmatory testing
Initiate specific targeted therapy (mineralocorticoid antagonist or adrenalectomy)
Monitor BP response and treatment efficacy

resistantHypertension.validation: True if BP uncontrolled on ≥3 meds including diuretic

potassiumLevel.validation: Normal or low - screening indicated regardless

validationRules: Resistant hypertension defined as BP uncontrolled on ≥3 antihypertensives including diuretic
Do not exclude based on normokalemia

rules: Identify adults with resistant hypertension (uncontrolled BP on ≥3 meds including diuretic)
Screen for primary aldosteronism regardless of potassium level (do not require hypokalemia)
Perform aldosterone-to-renin ratio as initial screening test
If screening positive, proceed to confirmatory testing
If confirmed, initiate specific targeted therapy (mineralocorticoid antagonist or adrenalectomy)
Goal: Increase detection rates and enable targeted treatment

suggestedPhrasing.description: Is recommended / Is indicated / Should be performed

loeDescription.description: Moderate-quality evidence from 1 or more well-designed nonrandomized studies, observational studies, or registry studies

recommendation.description: In adults with resistant hypertension, screening for primary aldosteronism is recommended regardless of whether hypokalemia is present to increase rates of detection, diagnosis, and specific targeted therapy

thresholds.sbp: N/A - screening recommendation, not acute crisis

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Screen all adults with resistant hypertension for primary aldosteronism regardless of potassium levels
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Follow up after screening results and after initiating targeted therapy
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Repeat screening if initially negative but clinical suspicion remains high
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag positive screening for confirmatory testing and specialist referral
nextStepRouting.documentation: Document screening results, confirmatory testing, and targeted therapy initiation
